attacking_style: Pure sprinter who excels in last 200-300m with perfectly timed jump, not reliant on early positioning. Shows tactical patience, often winning from 7th wheel at 1km to go, then launching simultaneously with rivals rather than following wheels.
team_tactics: Heavily dependent on Danny van Poppel's late leadout delivery (5+ instances), though capable of winning from chaos when leadout fails. Team positioning frequently disorganized in technical finishes and crosswinds, leaving him boxed or wrong side of splits.
terrain_preference: Best on uphill sprint finishes and power-suited gradients where pure speed matters less. Struggles on technical circuits with sharp corners and gets eliminated on even moderate climbs (Cat 3-4) despite team support.
vulnerability: Exposed in crosswinds and technical finishes with positioning errors (wrong side of splits, boxed at corners). Eliminated alongside pure sprinters (Jakobsen, Gaviria) on climbing stages. Train disintegrates on twisty finales. Mistimes headwind sprints.
key_patterns: Van Poppel leadout partnership produces wins across 2017-2022, critical tactical relationship
Positioning errors frequent: boxed corners, wrong splits, lost wheels on technical circuits (4 instances)
Uphill finish strength: won/placed on gradients beyond pure sprinter capability (3 occasions)
Gets dropped on climbs with other pure sprinters, predictable elimination pattern (2022-2019)
Wins from chaos/disorganized sprints when leadout absent, strong positioning instincts (2017-2019)
2023 shows train disintegration issues with Bora, fading despite good positioning
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 data shows consistent positioning but fading in final meters, suggesting speed decline or leadout coordination issues with new Bora setup versus peak 2019-2022 QuickStep/Vuelta form
